description_long: This object is a tall, slender candle with a cylindrical shape and a slightly irregular surface, suggesting it has been partially melted or weathered. The candle is predominantly a pale, off-white color with subtle blue-gray and darkened patches, possibly from soot or wax residue. The top of the candle is flat and appears unburned, with no visible wick or flame. The lower portion of the candle is uneven and jagged, as if it has broken or melted away, giving it a rough, unfinished look. There are no visible holders or decorative bases attached to the candle, and its surface texture appears matte with some minor glossiness in certain areas. This candle would likely be found as a decorative element in an urban outdoor setting, such as a park or public square.
description: A tall, slender, pale candle with a rough, irregular base and subtle blue-gray discolorations. The surface is mostly matte with some minor glossiness and no visible holder or flame.
description_view_0: The candle appears tall and cylindrical with a slightly jagged lower edge and a pale, off-white surface with a dark patch near the top.
description_view_1: This view highlights the candle's slender profile and a pronounced dark area near the lower section, with the top remaining flat and unburned.
description_view_2: The cylindrical shape is most evident here, with a smooth upper section and a rough, uneven base, as well as a blue-gray patch midway down.
description_view_3: The candle's surface appears mostly uniform in color, with a jagged lower edge and a darkened area near the top rim.
